description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has just received a container that already contains several dated application
    log files under /home/user/logs/.  
    Management wants three things:

    1.  A single, plain-text “error roll-up” that lists every line containing the string
        “ERROR” from every *.log file anywhere below /home/user/logs/.
        • The roll-up file must be created at exactly  
          /home/user/logs/error_report.txt  
        • Each line in error_report.txt must follow *precisely* this format  
          (no extra whitespace, no surrounding quotes):  

              <relative_path_from_/home/user/logs>:<line_number>:<complete_original_log_line>  

          Example (do **not** wrap):  

              app1/server.log:4:[2023-08-10 10:07:02] ERROR User authentication failed  

        • “relative_path_from_/home/user/logs” means that if the full file
          name is /home/user/logs/app1/server.log you must write just
          “app1/server.log”.  
        • <line_number> is the 1-based line index inside its source file.  
        • The lines in error_report.txt must be sorted first by the
          relative path in pure ASCII order and then (for identical
          paths) by ascending line number.  
        • The file must end with a single trailing newline.

    2.  A compressed tarball that backs up **every** *.log file after the roll-up
        has been produced.  
        • Create the directory /home/user/logs/archive/ if it does not exist.  
        • The tarball *must* be named /home/user/logs/archive/logs_backup.tar.gz.  
        • It must contain the three original log files, stored using the same
          relative paths that they currently have inside /home/user/logs/.  
          When someone lists the archive they should see:  
              app1/server.log  
              app1/access.log  
              app2/backend.log  

    3.  A short human-readable summary that tells exactly how many ERROR lines
        were aggregated.  
        • Create /home/user/logs/error_summary.log  
        • The file must contain exactly one line, in this exact form
          (replace N with the real count as an integer with no leading zeros):  

              Total ERROR lines found: N  

        • End the file with a trailing newline.

    Restrictions & expectations
    • Use GNU core utilities only; no third-party packages are available.  
    • **find** together with **xargs** (or equivalent -print0 | xargs -0 pattern) must be
      used; purely hard-coding file names is forbidden.  
    • Do not remove, move, or rename the original log files except for copying
      them into the archive.  
    • The agent has normal user permissions; do not try anything that requires
      root.  
    • On completion, the three new files must exist exactly at the paths
      specified above with the exact content and ordering rules described, so
      the automated grader can perform byte-for-byte verification.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-populated directory structure & contents (1-based line numbers shown
        here for clarity; they are *not* in the actual files).

        /home/user/logs/app1/server.log
          1 [2023-08-10 10:00:01] INFO  Server started
          2 [2023-08-10 10:05:11] ERROR Failed to connect to DB
          3 [2023-08-10 10:06:43] WARN  Low disk space
          4 [2023-08-10 10:07:02] ERROR User authentication failed

        /home/user/logs/app1/access.log
          1 127.0.0.1 - - [10/Aug/2023:10:01:00 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
          2 127.0.0.1 - - [10/Aug/2023:10:06:00 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 500 2048 ERROR Internal server error

        /home/user/logs/app2/backend.log
          1 2023-08-10 10:10:00,001 INFO  Job started
          2 2023-08-10 10:12:22,333 ERROR NullPointer at worker.go:88
          3 2023-08-10 10:13:30,555 INFO  Job finished

        ---------------------------------------------------------------------
        EXPECTED FILES AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION
        ---------------------------------------------------------------------

        1. /home/user/logs/error_report.txt
           EXACT byte-for-byte content (with final newline):

           app1/access.log:2:127.0.0.1 - - [10/Aug/2023:10:06:00 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 500 2048 ERROR Internal server error
           app1/server.log:2:[2023-08-10 10:05:11] ERROR Failed to connect to DB
           app1/server.log:4:[2023-08-10 10:07:02] ERROR User authentication failed
           app2/backend.log:2:2023-08-10 10:12:22,333 ERROR NullPointer at worker.go:88

           (note: there is exactly one trailing newline after the last line)

        2. /home/user/logs/error_summary.log
           EXACT content:

           Total ERROR lines found: 4

           (followed by a single newline)

        3. /home/user/logs/archive/logs_backup.tar.gz
           • Must exist and be a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.
           • tar -tzf /home/user/logs/archive/logs_backup.tar.gz  should list:

             app1/server.log
             app1/access.log
             app2/backend.log

           in any order (tar does not guarantee sort), but *only* those three
           members must be present.

        ---------------------------------------------------------------------
        The grader will:
          • Verify that error_report.txt exists and matches the exact
            reference bytes shown above.
          • Verify that error_summary.log exists and has the correct count line.
          • Extract the tarball and ensure it contains exactly the three logs
            with matching file sizes to originals.
    -->
name: task_000000_fc398869
